visual_checks: Look at all clickable elements (buttons, links, form controls) — are any visually small or difficult to target?
Check navigation menus, icon buttons, close buttons, and social media links for adequate size
Look for densely packed interactive elements where targets may overlap or be too close together
pass_conditions: All pointer input targets are at least 44x44 CSS pixels
Small targets have equivalent larger targets available on the same page
Inline links within sentences are excluded from size requirements
fail_conditions: Interactive elements are smaller than 44x44 CSS pixels without an equivalent larger target
Icon buttons or close buttons are too small to easily tap on touch devices
na_conditions: Page has no interactive elements that accept pointer input
common_mistakes: Icon-only buttons with no padding (just the icon size)
Close/dismiss buttons on dialogs that are very small
Social media icon links that are smaller than 44px
plain_meaning: This AAA rule requires pointer targets to be at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels. Exceptions are targets with an equivalent alternative, inline targets within a sentence, targets whose size is controlled by the user agent, and targets whose specific size is essential.
scope_applies_to: Buttons, links, and controls used with touch or mouse
Icon buttons, close buttons, and small controls
Custom controls where the author sets target size
scope_does_not_apply_to: Inline links within sentences of running text
Controls whose size is determined by the user agent (native form controls with default styling)
Targets with an equivalent larger alternative elsewhere on the page
Cases where the specific size is essential to the information being conveyed
borderline_calibration: This is the 44px AAA version; do not confuse with SC 2.5.8 (24px AA minimum).
Inline links in paragraphs are exempt regardless of size.
Only flag when the clickable area is clearly under 44x44 CSS pixels AND no exception applies.
User-agent default controls pass even if small.
